title: Au travail — Jobs & the workplace
objective: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: vouloir / pouvoir / devoir + a base verb
bridge.body: French has three handy verbs that work like English **want / can / must** + a base verb: **vouloir** (to want), **pouvoir** (can / to be able to), **devoir** (must / to have to). Each is followed by an infinitive: *Je **veux** travailler.* (I want to work), *Je **peux** venir.* (I can come), *Je **dois** partir.* (I must leave). It mirrors Hindi चाहना / सकना / पड़ना — one verb carries the modal meaning, then the main verb stays in its base form. For a general necessity use the impersonal **il faut** + infinitive (one must / it is necessary): *Il faut travailler dur.* This is an analogy, not a rule — and note **il faut** never changes, it is always **il**.

quiz.prompt: In French, what usually follows vouloir / pouvoir / devoir?
options: An infinitive (base verb), e.g. « Je veux travailler »
Always a noun, never a verb

example.french: Arjun est ingénieur dans une entreprise IT à Bangalore.
example.englishGloss: Arjun is an engineer at an IT company in Bangalore.

glosses.en: Arjun is an engineer at an IT company in Bangalore.
glosses.ta: அர்ஜுன் பெங்களூருவில் ஒரு தகவல் தொழில்நுட்ப நிறுவனத்தில் பொறியாளர்.
glosses.te: అర్జున్ బెంగళూరులో ఒక ఐటీ కంపెనీలో ఇంజనీర్.
glosses.kn: ಅರ್ಜುನ್ ಬೆಂಗಳೂರಿನ ಒಂದು ಐಟಿ ಕಂಪನಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ಎಂಜಿನಿಯರ್.

example.french: Priya est médecin dans un hôpital à Delhi.
example.englishGloss: Priya is a doctor at a hospital in Delhi.

glosses.en: Priya is a doctor at a hospital in Delhi.

example.french: Mon professeur de français est très gentil.
example.englishGloss: My French teacher is very kind.

glosses.en: My French teacher is very kind.

example.french: Rohan est comptable dans une startup à Gurgaon.
example.englishGloss: Rohan is an accountant at a startup in Gurgaon.

glosses.en: Rohan is an accountant at a startup in Gurgaon.

example.french: J'aime beaucoup mon travail à Bangalore.
example.englishGloss: I like my work in Bangalore a lot.

glosses.en: I like my work in Bangalore a lot.
glosses.ta: பெங்களூருவில் என் வேலையை நான் மிகவும் விரும்புகிறேன்.
glosses.te: బెంగళూరులో నా పనిని నేను చాలా ఇష్టపడతాను.
glosses.kn: ಬೆಂಗಳೂರಿನಲ್ಲಿ ನನ್ನ ಕೆಲಸವನ್ನು ನಾನು ತುಂಬಾ ಇಷ್ಟಪಡುತ್ತೇನೆ.

example.french: Le bureau à Gurgaon est grand et moderne.
example.englishGloss: The office in Gurgaon is big and modern.

glosses.en: The office in Gurgaon is big and modern.
glosses.ta: குர்கானில் உள்ள அலுவலகம் பெரியதும் நவீனமானதும் ஆகும்.

example.french: Nous avons une réunion importante à dix heures.
example.englishGloss: We have an important meeting at ten o'clock.

glosses.en: We have an important meeting at ten o'clock.
glosses.ta: எங்களுக்கு பத்து மணிக்கு ஒரு முக்கியமான கூட்டம் உள்ளது.
glosses.te: మాకు పది గంటలకు ఒక ముఖ్యమైన సమావేశం ఉంది.
glosses.kn: ನಮಗೆ ಹತ್ತು ಗಂಟೆಗೆ ಒಂದು ಮುಖ್ಯ ಸಭೆ ಇದೆ.

example.french: Arjun a un entretien d'embauche demain à Bangalore.
example.englishGloss: Arjun has a job interview tomorrow in Bangalore.

glosses.en: Arjun has a job interview tomorrow in Bangalore.
glosses.ta: அர்ஜுனுக்கு நாளை பெங்களூருவில் ஒரு வேலை நேர்காணல் உள்ளது.
glosses.te: అర్జున్‌కు రేపు బెంగళూరులో ఒక ఉద్యోగ ఇంటర్వ్యూ ఉంది.
glosses.kn: ಅರ್ಜುನ್‌ಗೆ ನಾಳೆ ಬೆಂಗಳೂರಿನಲ್ಲಿ ಒಂದು ಉದ್ಯೋಗ ಸಂದರ್ಶನ ಇದೆ.

exercises.hint: pouvoir, je form (can / to be able to).
exercises.coachNote: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)
exercises.prompt: Working from home: « Je ___ travailler à la maison. » (pouvoir)

exercises.hint: devoir, nous form (must / to have to).
exercises.coachNote: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)
exercises.prompt: « Nous ___ finir le projet avant la réunion. » (devoir)

exercises.coachNote: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)
exercises.prompt: « Priya ___ un nouvel emploi à Bangalore. » (vouloir, elle)

exercises.coachNote: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)
exercises.text: Pour réussir l'entretien, il {{}} arriver à l'heure.

exercises.coachNote: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)
exercises.text: Arjun {{}} aller au bureau à Bangalore aujourd'hui. (devoir)

exercises.coachNote: Talk about jobs, workplaces and obligations. (A2: can describe one's job and workplace and express simple obligations and wishes.)
exercises.explanation: une réunion = a meeting; un entretien = a (job) interview; un bureau = an office; un emploi = a job.

exercises.hint: Match each profession to its English name.
